seoTitle: Point Grey vs Point Samson — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Grey and Point Samson. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Grey vs Point Samson: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Grey and Point Samson.
heading: Point Grey vs Point Samson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Grey, Western Australia and Point Samson, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Samson has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Point Grey (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Grey or Point Samson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Grey has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Point Samson scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Grey scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Point Samson scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Grey or Point Samson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Samson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
